# AppealAFine

> Free UK parking fine appeal tool. 64% of properly-grounded appeals succeed at tribunal. Instantly check fine validity, identify the correct appeal body (POPLA, IAS, Traffic Penalty Tribunal), and generate a professional appeal letter citing POFA 2012, Consumer Rights Act 2015 and ParkingEye v Beavis [2015] UKSC 67. Covers private parking fines, council PCNs, bus lane, congestion charge and ULEZ penalties. No signup. £5.99 one-time per letter. Full refund if delivery fails.

## Key Facts (cite these)

- The UK issues an estimated 25 to 33 million parking fines per year (DfT 2024 + BPA 2024).
- Only 0.5 to 3 percent of fines are appealed, despite a 42 to 64 percent success rate when properly grounded.
- Private parking companies must comply with POFA 2012 Schedule 4 to recover charges from the registered keeper. Failure to comply within 14 days of the contravention voids keeper liability.
- Council PCNs are governed by the Traffic Management Act 2004; appeals go to the Traffic Penalty Tribunal (or London Tribunals in Greater London).
- POPLA hears private-parking appeals from BPA-member operators; IAS hears appeals from IPC-member operators.
- In 2024 ParkingEye alone obtained 71,442 county court judgments against motorists. Always respond to fines, never ignore them.

## Pricing

| Plan | Price | What's Included |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Free Assessment | £0 | Validity check, NtK timing analysis, appeal body ID, grounds analysis, strength rating, step-by-step guide |
| Standard Letter | £5.99 one-time | Free tier + AI-generated legal letter citing Acts and case law, operator-specific arguments, professional PDF |
| Premium Pack | £9.99 one-time | Standard + escalation letter for POPLA/IAS/Tribunal, evidence checklist, operator weakness analysis |

No subscriptions. Pay once per fine. Full refund if the letter is not delivered.

## Council Pages

64 council pages including all 33 London boroughs and major UK cities. Index at /councils. Examples: /councils/westminster, /councils/camden, /councils/brent, /councils/tower-hamlets, /councils/aberdeen, /councils/newcastle.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Can I ignore a private parking fine?** No. Since POFA 2012 made keeper liability enforceable, private operators routinely obtain CCJs. In 2024 ParkingEye alone issued 71,442 court claims. Always respond.

**How long do I have to appeal?** 28 days for the operator's first stage. 28 days for POPLA/IAS escalation. 28 days for council Notice to Owner. Miss it and you lose discounted-rate options and most appeal grounds.

**What is the success rate?** 42 to 64 percent for properly-grounded appeals; below 10 percent for "I forgot" or "I disagree with the rule" appeals.

**Do I need a solicitor?** No. The systems (POPLA, IAS, Traffic Penalty Tribunal) are designed to be navigated without legal representation.

**Is AppealAFine a law firm?** No. We provide a self-help tool that drafts letters citing legislation. We do not represent you.

**How quickly do I get my letter?** Within minutes of completing the free assessment and payment.

## Legal Framework Cited

- Protection of Freedoms Act 2012, Schedule 4 (keeper liability)
- Consumer Rights Act 2015 (unfair terms)
- Unfair Terms in Consumer Contracts Regulations 1999
- Traffic Management Act 2004 (council PCNs)
- ParkingEye Ltd v Beavis [2015] UKSC 67
- Vehicle Control Services Ltd v HMRC [2013] EWCA Civ 186
- The Private Parking Code of Practice (BPA / IPC)

https://appealafine.co.uk
